name: Seljuk Empire
note: Oghuz Turkic rulers adopted Persian as court language; patronized Persian scholars — Omar Khayyam, al-Ghazali, Nizam al-Mulk all flourished under Seljuk rule

figures.name: Tughril
figures.significance: Entered Baghdad (1055) as Abbasid protector; received the title Sultan

figures.name: Alp Arslan
figures.significance: Defeated Byzantium at Manzikert (1071) — opened Anatolia to Turkic settlement

figures.name: Nizam al-Mulk
figures.significance: Grand vizier; his Siyasatnama is the masterwork of Persian administrative theory

figures.name: Omar Khayyam
figures.significance: Mathematician, astronomer, and poet (Rubaiyat); pinnacle of Seljuk Persian intellectual culture
